rows.notes: Clear printed folio/ledger number at top centre. No monetary columns present on this line.

Amount columns (269 10 3) are well aligned and clear. Description handwriting is less certain — reads as a carried forward / paid forward line (I chose 'Solicitor's [Paid forward]' as a concise transcription).
Clear heading in larger script introducing the list of subscription entries; no amounts on this line.
Description and amount (6 6 0) are clearly legible and align with pounds/shillings/pence columns.

Amount (20 0 0) clear. Description partly uncertain: reads like 'Per Court School to Mrs. 1837' — the middle word could be 'Court' or 'Coast' or 'Par. Court'; chosen reading based on letter shapes and context.
Amount (5 5 0) clear. Place/name reads like 'Redington' followed by the abbreviation 'Do.' (ditto). Minor uncertainty in a single internal letter.
Amount (5 5 0) clear. Description reads 'St Newington' followed by 'Do' (ditto). Letters are legible though ornate.
Amount (6 0 0) clear. Description looks like 'Little Town Do' (the first word might be 'Little' or similar small-place name).
Amount (3 3 0) clear. Description reads as 'Garston' (G-a-r-s-t-o-n) with 'Do' abbreviation. Handwriting of first letter is slightly cramped but legible.
Amount (5 5 0) clear. First word appears to be 'Wharton' or 'Warton' (handwriting of 'h' small and ambiguous), followed by 'Do'.
Amount (5 5 0) clear. Name appears as an initialed person 'L. H. Blackburne' followed by 'Do'. The initials are inferred from capital shapes; surname reading is moderately certain.
Amount (5 0 0) clear. Description is hard to read — looks like 'Florencear' or 'Florencear/Flovencar'. I chose a best-fit transcription but the proper place/name is uncertain.

rows.description: Smale living in Diocese of Oxford
rows.notes: Amount (5 0 0) clear. Description appears to read 'Smale living in Diocese of Oxford' or similar phrasing — 'Smale' might be a surname or 'Small' with an ornate 'l'. The 'Diocese of Oxford' phrase is fairly clear.
Amount (2 0 0) clear. Description clearly reads 'The University Marshal' (handwriting legible for this formal title).
Amount (12 0 0) clear. Description reads as 'The Bell Ringer & Chapel Clerk' (ampersand and roles are legible).

rows.description: Rowe (?) for care of the Clock
rows.notes: Amount (5 5 0) clear. Surname appears as 'Rowe' or 'Rowel' for 'care of the clock' — the name's final letters are slightly ambiguous; function 'care of the clock' is legible.
Columns show 19 shillings 6 pence; pounds column blank so set to 0. Description reads like 'Copy Prices' or 'Coy Prices' — first letter uncertain but 'Prices' is clear.
Amount (1 1 0) and description 'Easter Offering' are both legible. Alignment with columns is clear.
Amount (10 0 0) clear. Description 'Parish Rates' readable and unambiguous.
Text reads 'Synodals & Procurations' but there is no amount on this line. The spelling is old-fashioned and the ampersand is used; absence of numbers is clear.
Amount (7 12 6) is well aligned and legible. Description is moderately clear: 'College Gardener - 1st Do to Xmas 1837' (interpreted 'Do' as 'ditto' style abbreviation).
Amount (7 13 0) clear. Line reads '2nd - to Ditto 1838' (the exact wording is abbreviated and the middle abbreviation was expanded to 'Ditto' for clarity).
There is a third line under the College Gardener heading with '3d -' and a short word that looks like 'Mid' or 'Mids.' but no distinct numeric amount on this line; the script is faint here.
Fourth line reads '4th Mich.' (likely a period label). There is no separate amount on this line; it appears grouped with the previous lines (faint handwriting).
A bracketed subtotal for the College Gardener lines is written as 18 10 0; alignment and digits are clear. I labeled this a 'total' because it is presented as the sum for the bracketed group.
Final page total at bottom right (L 401 0 3) is clearly written and aligned. Interpreted 'L' as the pound sign; this is the page total and thus a 'total' row.
